sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of mangosteens grown from Garcinia mangostana L., of the Guttiferae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mangosteens for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of mangosteens grown from Garcinia mangostana L., of the Guttiferae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mangosteens for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of mangosteens grown from Garcinia mangostana L., of the Guttiferae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Mangosteens for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS
CLASSIFICATION
sections.text: Mangosteens are classified in three classes defined below:

number=2.2
level=2
Mangosteens are classified in three classes defined below:

page_spans.text: Mangosteens are classified in three classes defined below:

sections.title: The development and condition of the mangosteens
path_titles: The development and condition of the mangosteens
sections.text:
The development and condition of the mangosteens must be such as to enable them:
­ to ensure a continuation of the ripening process until they reach the appropriate degree of ripeness (the skin should be at least of a pink colour);
­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Mangosteens in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Size is determined by the weight or the diameter of the equatorial section of the fruit, in accordance with the following table: Size Code Weight (in grams) Diameter (in millimetres) A 30 - 50 38 - 45 B 51 - 75 46 - 52 C 76 - 100 53 - 58 D 101 - 125 59 - 62 E > 125 > 62

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package (or in each lot for produce presented in bulk) for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

level=1

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
number=5
level=1

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Mangosteens in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: - slight defects in shape; - slight defects on the peel and calyx such as bruising, scratches or other mechanical damage. The total area affected shall not exceed 10%. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

The total area affected shall not exceed 10%. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: SIZE TOLERANCES
sections.text: For all classes , 10% by number or weight of mangosteens not satisfying the requirements as regards sizing, but falling within the size immediately above or below those indicated in Section 3.

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be uniform and contain only mangosteens of the same origin, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.text: Five percent by number or weight of mangosteens not satisfying the requirements of the class, but meeting those of Class I or, exceptionally, coming within the tolerances of that class.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Ten percent by number or weight of mangosteens satisfying neither the requirements of the class nor the minimum requirements, with the exception of produce affected by rotting or any other deterioration rendering it unfit for consumption.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text: Mangosteens must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Mangosteens shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44-1995).

MARKING OR LABELLING

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the mangosteens. Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk, these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

components.subject: mangosteens
criteria: with the calyx and pedicel intact
sound (excluding produce affected by rotting or deterioration)
clean, practically free of visible foreign matter
practically free of pests affecting appearance
free of abnormal external moisture (except condensation after cold storage)
free of any foreign smell and/or taste
fresh in appearance with shape, colour and taste characteristic

­ to withstand transport and handling; and
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR MANGOSTEENS (CODEX STAN 204-1997)
pages.summary: Página apresenta o padrão Codex Stan 204 para mangostões, com definição do produto e detalhes provisionais de qualidade, incluindo subtítulos e requisitos mínimos até a classificação 'Extra' que continua na próxima página. Hierarquia clara com níveis 1 a 3, texto associado corretamente, e marcação de conteúdo que segue na próxima página para continuidade.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 204
pages.summary: Página com hierarquia clara numerada destacando classes e provisões (2.2.2 Class I, 3, 4, 4.1, 4.1.1, 4.1.2, 4.2, 5, 5.1). Texto associado a cada nó foi devidamente atribuído com continuidade marcada para 2.2.2 Class I vindo da página anterior e 4.1.1 e seção 5 com continuação para a próxima página, para garantir qualidade na recuperação por similaridade (RAG).

pages.title: CODEX STAN 204
pages.summary: Página apresenta hierarquia clara numerada com duas seções principais 5.2 PACKAGING e 6 MARKING OR LABELLING, contendo subseções e subsubseções bem definidas. O texto foi devidamente associado a cada nó estrutural respeitando numeração e conteúdo. Não há continuidades indicadas entre páginas para os nós desta página.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 204
pages.summary: A página contém duas seções principais numeradas 7 CONTAMINANTS e 8 HYGIENE, cada uma com subseções 7.1, 7.2 e 8.1, 8.2 respectivamente. O texto de cada subseção está incluído no título devido à sua extensão e continuidade imediata, sem texto adicional separado na página. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas para essas seções. O cabeçalho e o título do documento foram identificados corretamente para suporte ao RAG.
